evals.prompt: Review these two files from a PR about service handling infrastructure: OpsObjects/PrimeInstall/components/ADS/install.yml and OpsObjects/PrimeInstall/modules/PrimeInstall.Service/PrimeInstall.Service.psm1. These were reviewed by a senior engineer who left 25+ comments. I want to see if the review skill catches the same categories of issues. Focus on error handling, consistency, module usage patterns, and deployment quality.
evals.expected_output: Should catch the same categories flagged in the original review: error handling issues (Write-Warning where throw is needed, broad try-catch scopes), consistency problems (duplicated validation logic, inconsistent data structures across similar functions), module usage (using $LASTEXITCODE instead of structured output from sc.exe), and pipeline task configuration ordering.
expectations: Flags error handling concern: Write-Warning used where the operation should throw a terminating error (Remove-PrimeServiceDependency line 177)
Flags error handling concern: broad try-catch scope that obscures which line actually throws the expected exception
Flags consistency concern: service name validation logic duplicated rather than extracted to a shared helper
Flags module usage concern: reliance on $LASTEXITCODE global variable instead of capturing sc.exe output in a structured way
Flags inconsistent data structures across similar functions (Get-PrimeServiceStatus vs Get-PrimeService return different shapes for similar data)

evals.prompt: Review the file OpsObjects/PrimeInstall/components/ATS/install.yml for infrastructure and deployment quality issues. Focus on error handling, security, and pipeline best practices.
evals.expected_output: Should flag: errorActionPreference SilentlyContinue without justification, condition: succeededOrFailed() running tasks despite prior failures, hardcoded network path in default parameter, hardcoded remote paths (D:\Temp\PrimeInstall\...), passwords assigned to inline variables.
expectations: Flags errorActionPreference SilentlyContinue as a concern (error suppression without justification)
Flags condition: succeededOrFailed() as unusual — tasks running after prior failures need explanation
Flags the hardcoded network share path in the licenseStoragePath default parameter
Flags hardcoded remote paths like D:\Temp\PrimeInstall as values that should be parameterized
Identifies password handling concerns (passwords passed through inline script variables)

evals.prompt: Review OpsObjects/PrimeInstall/components/ADS/install.yml and OpsObjects/PrimeInstall/components/AMBA/install.yml together. Focus on consistency between these two component installation files and any cross-cutting concerns.
evals.expected_output: Should flag: inconsistent template reference styles (relative vs absolute paths), different patterns for module path setup, different approaches to password handling, inconsistent use of condition guards, different error handling configurations.
expectations: Flags inconsistent template reference paths — ADS uses relative (../../shared/...) while AMBA uses absolute (/OpsObjects/PrimeInstall/shared/...@self)
Flags that AMBA duplicates PSModulePath setup across multiple tasks while ADS handles it differently
Flags differences in parameter handling between the two components that should follow a common pattern
Identifies that both files pass secrets as inline command arguments, which is a shared concern across components
